Reading a File in Rust

The standard library gives you everything you need to read a whole file into a string without pulling in an external crate. The example below reads the file at a path and prints its contents to standard output.

use std::fs;

fn main() {
    let text = fs::read_to_string("notes.txt").expect("read failed");
    println!("{text}");
}

Prefer fs::read_to_string for UTF-8 text and fs::read for raw bytes when the input is not guaranteed to be valid UTF-8, since the former returns an error on invalid encoding.
